ai_insights.executive_summary: NCS delivered solid operational momentum in February 2026, with both Legacy and Methodist clinics posting meaningful gains in scheduling discipline and closing-time performance. Legacy achieved a performance score of 66 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), while Methodist scored 76 out of 100, reflecting disciplined execution across core patient flow metrics. The network's scheduler compliance improvements—Legacy rising from 52.5% in January to 78.0% in February, and Methodist climbing from 57.2% in January to 82.7% in February—now position both clinics near or above the company average of 80.14%.
highlights: Legacy eliminated treatment overruns in February, reducing treatments past closing per day from 0.1 in January to 0.0 in February, a 100% improvement. Across both clinics, average patient delays narrowed meaningfully: Legacy's average delay fell from 5.5 minutes in January to 4.9 minutes in February (an 11.1% improvement), and Methodist's fell from 5.8 minutes in January to 5.2 minutes in February (a 10.4% improvement), with Methodist now tracking 0.14 minutes below the company average of 5.06 minutes. Both clinics are maintaining perfect iAssign adoption at 100%, which appears associated with solid nurse utilization outcomes—Legacy at 65.4% and Methodist at 69.3%.
concerns: We noticed Legacy's chair utilization sits at 61.1%, trailing the company average of 66.31% by 5.2 percentage points; this represents the network's clearest capacity opportunity. Methodist's chair utilization of 71.5% exceeds the company average, suggesting there may be scheduling or intake patterns at Legacy worth examining to better align chair deployment with patient demand. Alongside these observations, Methodist's minutes past close per patient improved substantially from 48.4 in January to 13.0 in February, but still carries residual overtime relative to Legacy's perfect 0.0; exploring what closure protocols Legacy refined might help Methodist close to perfect execution.
recommendations: We'd recommend Legacy conduct a targeted review of its scheduling templates and chair-assignment logic to close the 5.2-point utilization gap versus the company benchmark. A brief audit pairing the scheduler team with a nursing lead to map patient arrival patterns and treatment durations against current template slots may surface quick wins in throughput without adding complexity.

ai_insights.executive_summary: Both Legacy and Methodist demonstrated strong scheduling discipline gains in March 2026, with Scheduler Compliance rising to 89.2% and 94.6% respectively—both now tracking above or near the company average of 91.61%. Legacy achieved a Performance Score of 73 out of 100 (where 50 represents the network average), while Methodist reached 80 out of 100, reflecting solid operational momentum across the network. However, Methodist's treatment overtime metrics shifted unfavorably in March, warranting attention alongside continued gains in appointment adherence.
highlights: Legacy's Scheduler Compliance improved 14.3 percentage points from 78.0% in February 2026 to 89.2% in March 2026, alongside a 10.6-point gain in Chair Utilization from 61.1% to 67.6% over the same period—both movements suggest improved scheduling discipline is supporting better asset flow. Methodist's Scheduler Compliance similarly strengthened by 14.4 percentage points from 82.7% in February 2026 to 94.6% in March 2026, now exceeding the company average of 91.61% and positioning the clinic among our strongest performers on this metric.
concerns: We noticed Methodist's treatments running past closing time increased from 0.1 per day in February 2026 to 0.2 per day in March 2026, alongside a doubling of minutes past close per patient from 13.0 to 26.2 minutes over the same period; the data suggests that as Scheduler Compliance improved, patient volume or appointment density may have shifted, worth exploring together to understand whether this reflects volume growth or scheduling pattern changes. Legacy's average patient delay rose from 4.9 minutes in February 2026 to 5.6 minutes in March 2026 (now aligned with the company average of 5.54 minutes), a 14.1-point climb that coincided with stronger compliance gains—one area worth reviewing is whether the improved scheduling adherence has absorbed higher patient loads without proportional staffing adjustments.
recommendations: Legacy's Scheduler Compliance of 89.2% remains 2.4 percentage points below the company average of 91.61%, representing our largest performance gap among the two clinics. We recommend piloting a brief audit of Legacy's scheduling templates and iOptimize recommendation adherence patterns—specifically, reviewing the 10-12% of appointments that still deviate from iOptimize suggestions—to identify whether particular time slots, treatment types, or staff assignments drive non-compliance. This targeted review, paired with frontline scheduler feedback, should help close the remaining gap efficiently.

scheduler_compliance.label: Scheduler Compliance
scheduler_compliance.explanation: Percentage of appointments scheduled following iOptimize recommendations. Higher is better. Company average is typically 60-70%. OncoSmart benchmark target is 75%.

avg_delay_mins.label: Avg Delay
avg_delay_mins.explanation: Average daily schedule delay in minutes. Lower is better. Measures how far behind the clinic is running on average.

avg_treatments_per_day.label: Tx Past Close/Day
avg_treatments_per_day.explanation: Average treatments running past treatment close per day (overtime patients per day). Lower is better.

avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.label: Tx Mins Past Close/Patient
avg_treatment_mins_per_patient.explanation: Average treatment minutes past closing time per patient. Lower is better.

avg_chair_utilization.label: Chair Utilization
avg_chair_utilization.explanation: Average chair utilization rate. Higher is better.

iassign_utilization.label: iAssign Utilization
iassign_utilization.explanation: Percentage of nurse assignments completed via iAssign. Higher means more consistent, optimized assignments.

avg_patients_per_nurse.label: Patients/Nurse/Day
avg_patients_per_nurse.explanation: Average patients per nurse per day. Context-dependent -- too high means understaffing, too low means inefficiency.

avg_chairs_per_nurse.label: Chairs/Nurse
avg_chairs_per_nurse.explanation: Average chairs assigned per nurse. Context-dependent.

avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.label: Nurse Utilization
avg_nurse_to_patient_chair_time.explanation: Average nurse-to-patient in-chair time per day. Higher is better.

chatbot_context.data_notes: Company Avg rows represent the network average across all locations for this client in the given month. Onco rows are global oncology benchmarks from the OncoSmart network. Rows named Global Avg, Network Avg, Total, or Overall are network-wide aggregates, not individual clinic locations. MoM (Month-over-Month) deltas compare the current month to the prior month. Composite scores are weighted 0 to 100 where 100 is best-in-class performance.
